description: When the backend's algorithm does not match the adapter type, ::new() returns Err(SecretError::InvalidUri(...)), but this is not a URI parsing failure — the URI was already parsed successfully and a backend was constructed. What goes wrong: callers matching on SecretError variants to distinguish 'bad URI string' from 'algorithm mismatch' cannot do so; both map to InvalidUri. A dedicated variant (e.g. SecretError::AlgorithmMismatch or similar) or an existing semantically-correct variant should be used instead. This conflates two distinct error conditions under one variant.
title: Wrong error variant for algorithm mismatch
